title: hybrid-add-holes
answer:
### Conceptual Summary
This organogram represents a speculative idiophone inspired by the conceptual blending of a traditional wooden marimba and a modern digital synthesizer. The central symbol is a square (idiophone) with holes (aerophones) incorporated for enhanced resonance. The inferior attachment zone indicates a contact interface with mallets and includes a digital control input for electronic modulation. The lateral information zones encode the material composition (wood) and the social function (educational tool for music therapy).
summary:
### Conceptual Summary
This organogram represents a speculative idiophone inspired by the conceptual blending of a traditional wooden marimba and a modern digital synthesizer. The central symbol is a square (idiophone) with holes (aerophones) incorporated for enhanced resonance. The inferior attachment zone indicates a contact interface with mallets and includes a digital control input for electronic modulation. The lateral information zones encode the material composition (wood) and the social function (educational tool for music therapy).
materials_text:
### Materials
1. 4 wooden bars, 300 mm length, 50 mm width, 20 mm thickness
2. 16 metal pins, 10 mm diameter, 50 mm length
3. 8 wooden mallets, 150 mm length, 20 mm diameter
4. 1 digital controller, 100 x 50 x 20 mm
5. 80 rubber strips, 50 mm length, 10 mm width, 2 mm thickness

### Virtual Materials
1. Wood texture (PNG, 1024x1024)
2. Metal pin model (STL, 10 mm diameter, 50 mm length)
3. Wooden mallet model (STL, 150 mm length, 20 mm diameter)
4. Digital controller model (STL, 100 x 50 x 20 mm)
5. Rubber strip model (STL, 50 mm length, 10 mm width, 2 mm thickness)

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.patches import Polygon, Circle, PathPatch
from matplotlib.transforms import offset_copy
from matplotlib.transforms import Affine2D

# Organogram
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.style.use('dark_background')

# Family symbol (idiophone)
idiophone = Polygon(((0, 0), (0, 1), (1, 1), (1, 0)), color='orange')
ax.add_patch(idiophone)

# Holes (aerophones)
holes = [Circle((0.2, 0.3), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.8, 0.3), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.2, 0.7), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.8, 0.7), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.5, 0.2), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.5, 0.8), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.3, 0.5), 0.05, color='lightblue'),
         Circle((0.7, 0.5), 0.05, color='lightblue')]
for hole in holes:
    ax.add_patch(hole)

# Interface with mallets and digital control
interface = Polygon(((0, 0), (0, 0.5), (0.5, 0.5), (0.5, 0)), color='grey')
ax.add_patch(interface)
ax.annotate('Mallets', xy=(0.5, 0.25), ha='center', va='center', color='white')
ax.annotate('Digital Control', xy=(0.5, 0.75), ha='center', va='center', color='white')

# Performer position
performer = Circle((0.5, 0.2), 0.05, color='white')
ax.add_patch(performer)
ax.annotate('Performer', xy=(0.5, 0.15), ha='center', va='center', color='white')

# Arrows for connections
arrow1 = FancyArrowPatch((0.9, 0.5), (0.7, 0.5), arrowstyle='<->', mutation_scale=20, color='yellow')
ax.add_patch(arrow1)
arrow2 = FancyArrowPatch((0.2, 0.6), (0.4, 0.6), arrowstyle='<->', mutation_scale=20, color='yellow')
ax.add_patch(arrow2)

# Axes labels and legend
ax.set_xlabel('Acoustic Components', color='white')
ax.set_ylabel('Interface & Control', color='white')
ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax.tick_params(colors='white')
ax.set_xticks([])
ax.set_yticks([]

import trimesh
body = trimesh.creation.cylinder(radius=18, height=180, sections=48)
mouth = trimesh.creation.cylinder(radius=10, height=35, sections=32)
mouth.apply_translation((0, 0, 107.5))
base = trimesh.creation.box(extents=(24, 24, 10))
base.apply_translation((0, 0, -95))
mesh = trimesh.util.concatenate([body, mouth, base])
